concept: Pain / The Sharp Thing Within / Hurt Felt in Body or Heart
description: Pain: the body's sharp signal, and by extension the heart's. Namable at last — 'thelai' (balm), 'theala' (heal), 'nepha' (medicine), and 'phamo' (console) have all been treating a nameless patient. Physical first; emotional pain borrows it naturally.
sound_symbolism: 'ki' is the sharp point, and 'po'–'na' the steady dwelling-with — pain as something attended rather than fled. The sharpness is real; so is the ground under it.
grammatical_notes: Noun with 'phaelo': 'mia kipona phaelo.' (I feel pain.) Located: 'mia mua rokai kipona phaelo.' (I feel pain in my back.) The healer's question: 'thia kua kipona phaelo.' (Where do you feel pain?)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Pain is inevitable; suffering is pain plus resistance. The tradition's central distinction requires the word this entry provides.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Healing began with the patient's report; the healer's craft is only as good as the language pain can be described in.

tags.emotion: Hurt of heart borrowing the body's word
